name: Huéscar and Orce Loop
name_original: Galera, Orce y Huéscar — vuelta por los páramos de Almaida
description: Cycle 56.9 miles (91.5 km) through the Almaida paramos, connecting Orce and Huéscar, with 2716 feet (828 metres) of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling around Galera, located in the province of Granada, Spain, offers diverse landscapes and varied terrain for cyclists. The region features well-paved surfaces suitable for road bikes, with routes traversing areas like the Almaida paramos and hinting at river valleys. This part of Andalusia is known for its diverse geography, including mountain ranges and semi-arid zones, which contribute to a range of cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Galera The most popular road cycling route is Guys, Orce and Huescar - return through the Almaida paramos, a 56.9 miles (91.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate route features an elevation gain of 2,715 feet (828 meters) and explores the distinctive Almaida paramos. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is The three Reservoirs - circular via Bolera, Portillo and San Clemente, a difficult 67.7 miles (108.9 km) path. This challenging route includes an elevation gain of 4,546 feet (1,386 meters) and connects multiple reservoirs, offering varied scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Arrival in Galera – Cúllar loop from El Barranco, a 37.4 miles (60.2 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 43 minutes. This moderate loop features an elevation gain of 2,112 feet (644 meters). Road cycling around Galera is defined by varied terrain, well-paved roads, and scenic views of the surrounding landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging excursions. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Galera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of road cycling routes can I expect around Galera?
A: Galera, located in the province of Granada, Spain, offers a variety of road cycling routes with diverse landscapes and varied terrain. You'll find well-paved roads suitable for road bikes, traversing areas like the distinctive Almaida paramos and hinting at river valleys. The region's geography, including mountain ranges and semi-arid zones, contributes to a range of cycling experiences, from moderate rides to more challenging excursions.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Galera area?
A: There are 19 road cycling routes documented around Galera. These routes cater to various fitness levels, with 3 easy, 12 moderate, and 4 difficult options to choose from.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, Galera offers several difficult routes. One notable option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987061125/the-three-reservoirs-circular-via-bolera-portillo-and-san-clemente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The three Reservoirs - circular via Bolera, Portillo and San Clemente&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 108.9 km path with an elevation gain of 1,386 meters, connecting multiple reservoirs and offering varied scenery.

Q: What are some moderate road cycling routes around Galera?
A: For those looking for a moderate ride, Galera has several excellent options. Consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987355623/guys-orce-and-huescar-return-through-the-almaida-paramos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guys, Orce and Huescar - return through the Almaida paramos&lt;/a&gt;, a 91.5 km trail with an elevation gain of 828 meters. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrival in Galera – Cúllar loop from El Barranco&lt;/a&gt;, a 60.2 km loop with 644 meters of elevation gain, leading through the local countryside.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The road cycling routes around Galera offer glimpses of the region's natural beauty and historical sites. Routes often traverse areas like the distinctive Almaida paramos and hint at river valleys. You might pass through or near settlements such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5899483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galera&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5463101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cúllar&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5311929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéscar&lt;/a&gt;. Historical landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba of the Seven Towers&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish of Our Lady of the Annunciation&lt;/a&gt; are also nearby.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the Galera area?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Galera are designed as loops, offering a convenient circular experience. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987061125/the-three-reservoirs-circular-via-bolera-portillo-and-san-clemente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The three Reservoirs - circular via Bolera, Portillo and San Clemente&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350385332/valle-del-rio-galera-por-orce-circular-desde-huescar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Río Galera por Orce — circular desde Huéscar&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrival in Galera – Cúllar loop from El Barranco&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Galera?
A: The broader Andalusia region, where Galera is located, experiences diverse weather. Generally, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, especially in semi-arid zones, while winters are typically mild but can have cooler temperatures at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any shorter road cycling routes suitable for a quicker ride?
A: If you're looking for a shorter ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350385332/valle-del-rio-galera-por-orce-circular-desde-huescar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Río Galera por Orce — circular desde Huéscar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 49 km route with 452 meters of elevation gain, offering a good option for a quicker outing.

Q: What kind of terrain will I encounter on Galera's road cycling routes?
A: You will encounter varied terrain, characteristic of Andalusia's diverse geography. Routes feature well-paved roads, but the elevation profiles indicate rolling hills and climbs, particularly on longer routes. You can expect scenic views of mountain ranges and semi-arid landscapes, with some routes passing through distinctive areas like the Almaida paramos.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that start from Huéscar?
A: Yes, several road cycling routes are accessible from Huéscar, a nearby settlement. For instance, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350385332/valle-del-rio-galera-por-orce-circular-desde-huescar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Río Galera por Orce — circular desde Huéscar&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123600" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéscar Historic Center – Huéscar loop from Huéscar&lt;/a&gt;, both offering moderate challenges and scenic views.

Q: What is the elevation gain like on the road cycling routes around Galera?
A: The elevation gain varies significantly across routes, catering to different fitness levels. Moderate routes typically feature elevation gains between 450 meters and 830 meters, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987355623/guys-orce-and-huescar-return-through-the-almaida-paramos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guys, Orce and Huescar - return through the Almaida paramos&lt;/a&gt; with 828 meters. More difficult routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987061125/the-three-reservoirs-circular-via-bolera-portillo-and-san-clemente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The three Reservoirs - circular via Bolera, Portillo and San Clemente&lt;/a&gt;, can have over 1,380 meters of climbing.
tips: Huéscar is a town located at the foot of the Río Huéscar and where it is close to the Sierra Bermeja, close to the Sierra de Segura a little further north.
Natural place to enjoy the mountains

Orce is one of the most beautiful towns to visit in the north of the province of Granada. It is characterized by its constructions, such as the Parish of Santa María and the well-known Alcazaba de las Siete Torres, with the famous seven pillars.
Road that approaches the center of Galera from where you drive through a set of hills and agricultural fields without tree vegetation and gray land.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' and requires 'good fitness'. It covers 91.5 kilometers with approximately 830 meters of elevation gain, making it a challenging yet rewarding experience for cyclists with some experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect mostly well-paved surfaces that are easy to ride. The route features varied terrain, including rolling hills and the expansive views of the Almaida paramos, which are high, windswept plateaus.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: The route connects the towns of Guys, Orce, and Huéscar. While specific parking details for a single trailhead are not provided, you can typically find parking within these towns to begin your ride. Consider starting in a town like Huéscar or Orce.

faqs.question: Is this a loop route?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is designed as a loop, starting and ending in the general area of Guys, Orce, and Huéscar, and returning through the Almaida paramos. This allows you to experience the diverse landscapes without retracing your path.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route connects several interesting locations. You'll pass through the town of Huéscar and near the Alcazaba of the Seven Towers of Orce. The route also offers views of the Sierra de María-Los Vélez Natural Park and concludes with the Arrival in Galera.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region of Andalusia can experience hot summers. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, with comfortable weather for tackling the route's distance and elevation.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery will I encounter?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse and striking landscapes characteristic of inland Andalusia. Expect views of natural parks, rugged massifs, valleys, and potentially distant snowy peaks in winter. The route also passes through areas with varied vegetation, including Aleppo pine and holm oaks, and offers glimpses of agricultural and historical landscapes around the towns.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily a road cycling route. While dogs are generally allowed in many outdoor areas, it's best to keep them off the road for their safety and the safety of cyclists. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are securely carried or that you stick to designated dog-friendly paths if you deviate from the main road.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route, especially within the Sierra de María-Los Vélez Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits or entrance fees are required for road cycling on public roads in this region. While the route offers views of the Sierra de María-Los Vélez Natural Park, you are cycling on established roads. However, always respect local regulations, especially if you venture off-road into the park itself, where specific rules for activities might apply.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food or accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route connects the towns of Guys, Orce, and Huéscar, you will find opportunities for food, drinks, and accommodation within these settlements. These towns offer a chance to experience local culture and history, and resupply during your ride.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route briefly intersect with other paths. You'll find it crosses parts of the Andalucía - South Route (Provincia de Granada), Ruta BTT de la película "Intemperie", El Camino Espiritual del Sur, and various segments of the Gran Senda Primeros Pobladores.
